sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content: Very Early and Early Stage Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC)

flowcharts.title: Very Early and Early Stage Hepatocellular Carcinoma (HCC)

nodes.label: Solitary HCC ≤2 cm (very early) OR within Milan criteria (single tumor ≤5 cm or ≤3 tumors each ≤3 cm, no macrovascular invasion, no extrahepatic spread) AND Child-Pugh A or B class AND ECOG PS 0-1.
Curative resection - Resection is the first-line curative option for patients with sufficient functional liver reserve (typically Child-Pugh A, without portal hypertension) [2].

Radiofrequency ablation (RFA) or Microwave ablation (MWA) - Ablation is an effective curative therapy, particularly for tumors ≤3 cm, and is the standard of care for patients not candidates for resection [3].

Liver transplantation - Transplantation is a curative option that treats both the tumor and the underlying cirrhosis. It is indicated for patients with HCC within Milan criteria who are not optimal surgical candidates due to portal hypertension or poor liver function [4].
HCC within Milan criteria but unresectable due to tumor location, insufficient liver reserve, or patient comorbidities; or awaiting transplant.
Transarterial chemoembolization (TACE) - TACE is the mainstay for unresectable HCC without vascular invasion or extrahepatic spread. It can downstage tumors to within transplant criteria [5].
Radiofrequency ablation (RFA) or Microwave ablation (MWA) - Ablation remains a curative-intent option for unresectable very early/early HCC.

edges.label: preferred if resectable and adequate liver reserve
preferred for very early HCC or as alternative to resection in patients with high surgical risk
excellent option for eligible patients meeting Milan criteria and with decompensated cirrhosis
standard locoregional therapy for intermediate stage (BCLC B), but can be used as bridge to transplant or for downstaging

nodes.label: Multinodular HCC without macrovascular invasion or extrahepatic spread, Child-Pugh A or B class, ECOG PS 0.
Transarterial chemoembolization (TACE) - TACE is the recommended first-line treatment for intermediate-stage HCC (BCLC B) [5].

Systemic therapy for TACE-refractory disease - For patients with TACE-refractory disease or those who develop vascular invasion/extrahepatic spread, systemic therapy with atezolizumab plus bevacizumab or other regimens is indicated.
HCC with macrovascular invasion (e.g., portal vein tumor thrombus) but no extrahepatic metastases.
Systemic therapy - Systemic therapy is recommended for HCC with macrovascular invasion. The preferred first-line regimen is atezolizumab plus bevacizumab [6].
Transarterial radioembolization (TARE) or selected TACE - Selective locoregional therapies may be considered for selected patients with segmental portal vein involvement, but data are limited.

edges.label: standard of care for this stage
for patients who progress on or are ineligible for TACE
preferred due to high risk of microvascular spread
may be considered in selected cases
